verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A encodes the inducible HSP70-1 chaperone, canonically cytosolic/nuclear, with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, surface display of HSP70 family members (including HSPA1A/HSP72) is well-documented in multiple non-baseline contexts: (1) Cancer/oncogenic state — tumor cells constitutively display HSP70 on the outer leaflet, a well-characterized ecto-chaperone phenomenon exploited by NK-cell-activating antibodies and CAR-T programs; (2) Cell death/ICD — HSP70 externalizes during immunogenic cell death as a damage-associated molecular pattern; (3) Stress-induced — heat shock and other stressors induce rapid PM translocation of the cytosolic HSP70 pool. These documented ecto-pools are not reversible wash-off recruitment; surface biotinylation and flow cytometry studies on tumor cell lines confirm stable surface residence. No TM anchor is involved — the mechanism is cell-state-induced outer-leaflet translocation. Narrow germline restriction and lysosomal exocytosis do not apply. This firmly qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced.
